title: synaphea petiolaris subsp. triloba
intro: Synaphea petiolaris subsp. triloba, also known as the Western Australian Synaphea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Boraginaceae, native to Western Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Its health benefits are attributed to its ability to relieve fever, coughs, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic. Synaphea petiolaris subsp. triloba can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, making it a versatile plant for various herbal preparations.
